item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is not something I was expecting to read today! I absolutely love it!

+1

item.title: Re: +100
item.content:encoded:
I hope you know that there is someone who will do that and upload it to YouTube.

and that someone will be me if i remember to.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Good point, fixed

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: One of my favorite SCPs so far. It's a bit whacky but that's what makes it unique for me without stopping it from being creepy. As a newbie I wouldn't change anything except for one thing. Correct me if I'm wrong but, wouldn't SCP-5991-1's stomach be measured in km^3 instead of km^2 as it isn't a 2 dimensional plain?
Go ahead, eat the bible, see what happens

item.title: Re: Great, but shouldn't it be a joke?
item.content:encoded:
No, because the author had planned this SCP to be a mainlister. And a mainlist SCP can be funny. So, it's a mainlist SCP and it will remain like that. It's not an horror site anymore, as long as the thing is good written, you can do a skip in all the genres and styles you want.

Forgive me if I sound a little bit harsh, but this type of comments is really common in the discussion pages and I really wish that one day, people will understand that not all skips have to be super serious or dark or things like that.

item.title: Great, but shouldn't it be a joke?
item.content:encoded:
I enjoyed this and I upvoted it. It's quite hilarious and well written.

This might be my inexperience on this site, but shouldn't this be a joke SCP?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
In my country, there is a myth that if you eat a book that you wish to remember, you will remember the contents better. It's a cliché scene in comedy and drama here where a students eats up a whole dictionary to learn English
Reminded me of that.
+1 for presumably unintended nostalgia.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
i wonder how the first one found out about this.
i mean who would get the idea to eat a book?

upvote

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Nope. Nope nope. Nopity nope nope nope.

Angry upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I was about to just comment with what and ask a LARGE series of questions. But then I thought of something far better and that is:

I can't believe I ate the whoooole thing.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It's a compelling premise but I feel the execution in the middle of the piece is less engaging than it could've been. The consumption -> boardwalk -> orange sac flow to enter seems unnecessarily complicated, and as far as I can tell there isn't some religious analogy or other information to make it meaningful in context.

Additionally after the reveal about this being a weird theme park, it doesn't sufficiently use that momentum to build up to a satisfying ending. Like I don't see any see any reason the paragraph starting with "The food court contains 40 different tables …" couldn't be rearranged or cut without any impact on the impact.
Easily my weirdest piece yet, thank you all!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This wiki is a strange place sometimes.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I remember when you came up with this one. Very good concept and very good execution. JesusVore leads to good abstract horror.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This seems like two excellent ideas:

1) The idea that eating a Bible produces an anomalous effect.

2) The creepy Jesus theme park.

IMO, the first one is the best idea. I like anomalies that are "everyday" but haven't been discovered until now. How long have Bibles been able to do this? How many people know? How can we possibly prevent this? It's genius.

The creepy theme park thing isn't my cup of tea, but still good. I do like the alternative interpretation of Jesus and the mysterious evacuation plans.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I too wish to be Vored by the Lord.

+1? The actual meat of the article doesn't do too much for me, but I like Vore Jesus and the absurdity of having to eat an entire bible to trigger him enough to just barely make it land.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That's a fair point, flash fiction is awesome.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I have to disagree with the other commenters regarding the lack of an exploration log; I much prefer the brevity this article has right now, though that could be do to my recent craving for flash-fiction.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Didn't look over the whole article, but I would also change this:

SCP-5991-1 will approach the subject and unhinge its own jaw. SCP 5991-1 will violently shove the subject down its throat and into its stomach

To

SCP-5991-1 will approach the subject and unhinge its own jaw. SCP-5991-1 will then violently shove the subject down its throat and into its stomach,

Don't know of it's just me, but I think that would make reading that paragraph slightly smoother. I put this in a reply so if Westrin sees your post he'll probably see this as well.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I feel the same as most in this thread in terms of how details were presented, but I’m upvoting just on the imagery and concept alone.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like how you addressed my main concerns with how the place was described. pastarasta does have a very good point in that an exploration log could have likely conveyed these details better, but as is, I feel good about upvoting it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Vore Jesus

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I enjoyed this a lot, especially the mouth Jesus, which is a fun and freaky image.

1+

My only criticism would be that some of the details would seem better fit in an exploration log than an article.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I get what the article is going for, but personally it doesn't do it for me. It's too wacky. There are also too many extraneous details, which, I guess, could be necessary if one was going to describe the place, and they probably reference certain things. If I don't see it that's on me.
-1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
+ SPaG stuff
- SPaG stuff

However the chances of SCP-5991's activation requirements

However, (add comma)

"The Edwards-Francis Agreement" was passed with the Vatican government, which state that

states that

unauthorized information regarding SCP-5991 and its triggers are to be removed

is to be removed

SCP-5991 will not trigger if the subject consumes a bible as well as another substance

Bible (capitalise)

SCP-5991-1 will approach the subject and unhinge their jaw, allowing it to consume a human without difficulty.

Inconsistent pronouns there - I suppose this also feels redundant in view of the following sentence?

SCP-5991-2 themed memorabilia

SCP-5991-2-themed memorabilia (hyphenate)

The children's books describe events that never occurred within the old or new testaments

Old or New Testaments (capitalise)

a large approximately 50kg pile of animal feces can be found

I'd suggest rewording to "a large pile containing approximately 50 kg of animal feces can be found" or something to that effect!

Analysis of the feces suggest that the animal that created it

suggests.

Well, this is kind of oddball. I guess the article is played fairly straight, but the honey in the bindings is a delightful thing to ponder, and there's a wry irony to there being an unfinished church inside this thing. Lovely!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
You already winned me with the "eating a whole bible" and "mouth Jesus" thing.
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Ah, yes, I remember this in the SCPD Discord. It was quite a fun read, really. I did remember that I kept a straight "wtf" face (in a good way) all the way through. Good work!

item.title: Author Post
item.content:encoded:
Enjoy the perfect Christian experience with the Lord!

I've been working on this one for a bit longer than usual and it's been in my sandbox collecting dust during jamcon, so I've decided now's a better time than never to release it.

Thanks to everyone who read this, I don't remember you all, but I do remember Brewsterion 1017, Deadly Bread, JustBixby and Tanhony. If I forgot you, please bug me and I'll add you in.

Oh, and huge thanks to pastarasta1 for coming up with the title.

Art

Vore Jesus
by barredowl

More of their stuff can be found here

Jesus B-Day Eggs, only $343!
by EpsilonScott
Jesus Bobbleheads 50% Off! (Content Warning)
by Deadspace123

More of their stuff can be found here

His Holy Maw (Content Warning)
by deadspace123

More of their stuff can be found here
